title_en: Overview of SI/PI Simulation: Active High-Speed Signals, Impedance, and Filtering Solution Analysis
text:
Even with robust component choices, a system's electrical performance can be undermined by its interconnect. Signal integrity (SI) and power integrity (PI) are not optional; they dictate system reliability and data transmission quality.

This post outlines critical SI/PI simulation strategies for high-speed designs.

⚡ **High-Speed Interconnect Channels:** Analysis of S-parameters and eye diagrams for reliable data links.
📊 **Bus Topology & Timing:** Simulation of topology, timing, and crosstalk to prevent signal degradation.
⚙️ **Power Plane Impedance:** Evaluating and optimizing power plane impedance and capacitor placement for stable power delivery.
🔋 **DC Voltage Drop:** Analyzing current carrying capacity and voltage/current density distribution within power planes.

Accurate SI/PI simulation during design phases directly impacts system bit error rates and overall operational stability, reducing costly rework cycles. Our team at JYTech has been building industrial-grade hardware, including AI boxes and energy management controllers, with this level of design rigor through our Sienovo product line.

Full write-up: https://sienovo.jytech.us/blog/128405320

What's your biggest SI/PI challenge in current designs?
